step_by_step_thinking:
Connective tissue graft placement has varying degrees of efficacy in terms of achieving full root coverage, especially in more extensive recession cases. According to the articles reviewed, Class IV receding gum line defects tend to present complex challenges because they encompass larger areas of recession extending beyond simple localized defects addressed effectively by traditional connective tissue graft placements. Therefore, considering the limitations highlighted regarding comprehensive coverage in broader or deeper recession scenarios, the graft placement will likely fail in Class IV recession cases.
